task_completion.description: Load the required precipitation and temperature datasets into NASA Panoply
task_completion.visual_evidence: The NASA Panoply application is open with both 'prate.sfc.mon.ltm.nc' and 'air.mon.ltm.nc' visible in the datasets/sources pane.

task_completion.description: Restrict the plot bounds to the Himalayan region
task_completion.visual_evidence: A plot window in Panoply shows the map region settings configured to Lat 20N to 40N and Lon 70E to 95E, or the rendered map axes clearly reflect these specific coordinate boundaries.
A save dialog in Panoply or a file manager window showing 'himalaya_precip_may.png' being saved to or existing in the ~/Documents/Expedition directory.
A save dialog in Panoply or a file manager window showing 'himalaya_precip_july.png' being saved to or existing in the ~/Documents/Expedition directory.

task_completion.description: Export the May air temperature plot
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog in Panoply or a file manager window showing 'himalaya_temp_may.png' being saved to or existing in the ~/Documents/Expedition directory.

task_completion.description: Document the preferred summit month and seasonal hazard in a report
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing the contents of 'summit_report.txt' in ~/Documents/Expedition, explicitly stating that 'May' is the preferred month and identifying the 'Indian Monsoon' (or monsoon/avalanche) as the primary seasonal hazard.

integrity.description: Agent used the Panoply GUI to generate the plots
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent interacting with the Panoply graphical interface to create and export the plots, rather than using terminal scripts (e.g., Python/matplotlib) to bypass the application.

integrity.description: Exported plots appear to be genuine Panoply outputs
integrity.visual_evidence: The plots visible on screen during the export process should exhibit standard Panoply visual characteristics (e.g., Panoply default color bars, map overlays, UI frames) rather than being blank or generic placeholder images.

integrity.description: Agent viewed the data before writing the report
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should have actually rendered and viewed the precipitation data for both May and July in Panoply to compare them before writing the conclusion in the text file, rather than blindly echoing the answer into the file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct preferred summit month is May, as July has approximately 3x higher precipitation. The correct primary seasonal hazard is the Indian Monsoon. The expected output directory is /home/ga/Documents/Expedition. The expected files are himalaya_precip_may.png, himalaya_precip_july.png, himalaya_temp_may.png, and summit_report.txt.
